filter_index.video_title: Pangarchulla - A Challenging Summit Climb From A Trekker's Eyes | Indiahikes | By Pritish Bhanushali

snow_or_rain_notes: Hard snow above 12,000 ft in April
Snow from Auli to summit in April

terrain_challenges: Overhanging narrow trail for about 1 km overlooking the Dhak valley when approaching Tali from Gorson Bugyal
Last 4-5 hours of summit day on snow over big boulders
Ridge section composed of 6 small ridge sections with ascending and descending portions requiring careful footing
Narrow and windy ridge near the summit
altitude_or_weather_risks: Considerable elevation gain on the first day can make trekkers more prone to AMS
Maximum altitude reached is 15,069 ft on the summit day
Rainfall or snowfall lasting for hours can change the entire trek dynamic
Rain can flood trails or make them too slushy to walk safely on
Snowfall can obstruct previously made paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week plan provided for currently inactive trekkers

experience_themes: Spring season with snow melting giving way to lush meadows
Snow present from Auli all the way to the summit
Campsites free of snow with hard snow deposited above 12,000 ft
primary_watchouts: Before April: too much snow for summit climb
After April: snow melts leaving exposed boulders with large gaps, making traversing exhausting and unsafe
Summit climb only safe in the small April window
Summit climb outside April (too much snow before, unsafe exposed boulders after)
Trekkers seeking a longer trekking season (summit climb window is only one month)
Overhanging narrow trail for about 1 km overlooking the Dhak valley when approaching Tali from Gorson Bugyal
best_for: Experiencing spring blooms (Rhododendrons and flowering meadows)
Trekkers seeking a snow trek with manageable snow conditions
not_ideal_for: Summit climb outside April (too much snow before, unsafe exposed boulders after)
Trekkers seeking a longer trekking season (summit climb window is only one month)
